title: India gold market update: Mixed demand signals
gist: In June 2026 Indian gold demand sent mixed signals: a sharp ~10-11% price drop revived jewellery buying and drove gold ETF and digital-gold inflows even as bar/coin demand cooled, while a May import-duty hike and surging old-gold recycling pushed imports to a one-year low and kept the domestic market at a discount.

themes.theme: Gold price correction and its drivers

insights.claim: The international (LBMA PM) gold price fell more than 11% in June 2026 to around US$4,000/oz, its lowest level since October, while the domestic MCX price declined roughly 10% to near INR141,000/10g, a six-month low.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: A sharp, synchronized correction resets entry points; watch whether the ~US$4,000/oz level holds as support after the partial recovery.
insights.quote: The international price fell by more than 11% to around US$4,000/oz, its lowest level since October

insights.claim: Despite the June drop, the domestic Indian gold price was up around 6% year-to-date while the international price was nearly 7% lower YTD, a divergence explained by a 9% import-duty hike in May and rupee depreciation against the US dollar.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Local investors are partly insulated from dollar-gold weakness by duty and currency effects, so Indian demand signals can decouple from global price trends.
insights.locator: In contrast, domestic price is up around 6% y-t-d

insights.claim: A stronger US dollar, intensifying expectations of US rate hikes, and a rotation toward equities in Western markets were cited as the forces pressuring gold prices lower.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: The bear case for gold hinges on hawkish Fed pricing and equity risk-appetite; a reversal in either could quickly re-support prices.
insights.quote: A stronger US dollar, intensifying expectations of US rate hikes, and a rotation towards equities in Western markets have weighed on gold prices.

insights.claim: Domestic gold discounts to the landed price narrowed from a peak of nearly US$150/oz in May to around US$20/oz in early July before widening back to US$40/oz by mid-July, signaling a gradual normalization of market conditions.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Discount levels are a real-time gauge of local supply-demand balance; persistent discounts point to soft demand and suppress fresh import needs.
insights.quote: Discounts averaged around US$20/oz to the landed price during the first two weeks of July, significantly lower than the peak discount of nearly US$150/oz recorded in May.
insights.locator: Ample supply keeps domestic prices at a discount

insights.claim: A surge in old-gold exchange since the mid-May duty hike is a primary supply source, with exchange volumes reported up a further 10-20% and some retailers indicating old-gold exchanges now account for as much as 70% of jewellery sales.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Recycled supply substitutes for imports, meaning strong jewellery sales no longer translate one-for-one into physical import demand.
insights.quote: Retailers report that exchange volumes have risen by a further 10–20%, with some indicating that old gold exchanges now account for as much as 70% of jewellery sales.
insights.locator: Meanwhile, the exchange of old gold

insights.claim: After a month-long demand lull from mid-May to mid-June, consumer buying began recovering in recent weeks led primarily by jewellery, stimulated by the price pullback, relative price stability, and retailer promotions.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Price dips act as a demand catalyst in India; watch the August festive season, for which manufacturers are already receiving order bookings.

insights.claim: The demand lull was driven partly by policy measures and a Prime Minister's appeal to limit gold purchases, alongside seasonal softness and an inauspicious period (Adhik Maas, 17 May to 15 June 2026).
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Political and cultural signals materially move Indian gold demand, an idiosyncratic risk factor for the world's second-largest market.

insights.claim: Major listed jewellery retailers including Titan, Kalyan Jewellers, Senco Gold and PN Gadgil reported April-June revenue growth broadly in the high 30-60% y/y range, aided by regional festivals, the summer wedding season and Akshaya Tritiya.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Listed jewellers are gaining formalized market share and are a cleaner equity proxy for Indian gold demand than physical import data.
insights.quote: Revenue growth was broadly in the high 30–60% y/y range, supported by regional festivals, the summer wedding season and Akshay Tritiya

insights.claim: Listed jewellers continued aggressive store expansion, adding between 8 and 33 stores each during the quarter, which the World Gold Council reads as confidence in the medium-term jewellery demand outlook.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Store-count momentum is a forward indicator of organized-retail confidence even amid subdued near-term consumer demand.
insights.quote: The continued pace of store openings can be seen as reflecting industry confidence in the medium-term outlook for jewellery demand.
insights.locator: These retailers continued with their store expansions

insights.claim: For listed jewellers, old-gold exchange for new jewellery averaged 43-55% of sales during the April-June quarter, a structural shift in how organized retail sources metal.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Roughly half of organized jewellery sales now recycle existing gold, dampening incremental import demand from a booming retail top line.
insights.locator: Old gold exchange for new jewellery

insights.claim: Indian gold ETFs recorded net inflows of INR34.4bn (US$356mn) in June, the highest since February and bucking the global outflow trend, with holdings up 2.2t to 119t and 135k new folios added, taking total accounts to 12.5 million.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Indian retail treats price dips as buying opportunities and is broadening participation, a structurally growing bid distinct from Western ETF flows.
insights.quote: Net inflows during the month were INR34.4bn (US$356mn), the highest since February.

insights.claim: Digital gold purchases via UPI rebounded to a three-month high in June, with transaction value up 4% m/m to INR25.5bn (US$269mn) and estimated volumes up 9% m/m to 1.7t, above the 17-month average of 1.4t.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Digital rails are a resilient, expanding accumulation channel for small-ticket Indian investors, worth tracking as a demand leading indicator.

insights.claim: Investment demand diverged by channel, as softer prices cooled bar and coin demand, which is typically bought during rising-price periods, even while ETF and digital-gold inflows strengthened.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Bar/coin buyers chase momentum while ETF/digital buyers buy dips; the split reveals different investor psychologies within the same market.
insights.quote: softer prices have tempered demand for bars and coins, which are typically bought for investment purposes and tend to attract stronger interest during periods of rising prices.
insights.locator: At the same time, softer prices

insights.claim: Gold imports fell for a second consecutive month to US$1.97bn in June, down 42% m/m and the lowest since June 2025, with estimated volumes of 16-22t versus 29t in May and 25t in June 2025.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Collapsing imports reflect soft demand, high supply-chain inventories and recycled supply rather than a bullish signal; it eases India's trade-deficit pressure.
insights.quote: At US$1.97bn, imports were down 42% m/m and the lowest since June 2025.

insights.claim: Gold's share of India's total merchandise imports fell to just 3% in June, well below the 17% recorded in January, even as import value was 7% higher y/y purely because the average landed cost of gold rose 38% from a year earlier.
insights.speaker: Kavita Chacko
insights.so_what: Higher prices mask sharply lower volumes; value-based import data overstates physical demand, a trap for analysts reading the trade numbers.
insights.quote: gold’s share of total merchandise imports fell to 3% in June, well below the 17% recorded in January.
